2026-06-15-v1.47-reflection-loop-finding.title: Hotfix — master_decision_timeout::repeated_sweeps_remain_stable PK collision flake (enqueue_review_master_schedule mints RVM<ts> with no collision counter)
2026-06-15-v1.47-reflection-loop-finding.source: dev pre-implement discovery + .mstar/AGENTS.md pre-existing claim verification
2026-06-15-v1.47-reflection-loop-finding.closure_note: V1.48 P0 T0: H-1 hotfix performed in-plan. RVM schedule_id PK collision root-caused: enqueue_review_master_schedule minted RVM<ts_ms> which collided when two opt-in Works enqueued in same millisecond. Fix: per-process RVM_COUNTER AtomicU32 appended as 6-hex suffix (mirrors ACH_COUNTER / R-V139P0-W-B). Regression test rvm_schedule_ids_are_unique_within_same_millisecond added.

2026-06-17-v1.49-findings-lifecycle.title: CLI `creator run` client-side findings fetch still uses `?status=open`, missing V1.49 `triaged` actionable set
2026-06-17-v1.49-findings-lifecycle.note: V1.49 P0 widened DAO SQL + auto_chain consumer; CLI client-side fetch at run.rs::assemble_open_findings_block still hard-codes `?status=open`. Suggested fix at V1.50: extend Local API ListFindingsQuery.status to accept comma-separated list (or add `:actionable` endpoint) + update CLI to pass `open,triaged` + CLI integration test. Auto-chain path (production) is unaffected — already uses widened DAO filter.

2026-06-17-v1.49-findings-lifecycle.title: Pre-existing `cargo clippy --all -- -D warnings` failure on clean V1.49 integration HEAD (local toolchain drift)
2026-06-17-v1.49-findings-lifecycle.source: P0 fix-wave implementer (out-of-scope finding); verified pre-existing per `.mstar/AGENTS.md` protocol
2026-06-17-v1.49-findings-lifecycle.scope: crates/nexus-contracts/src/local/schedule/http.rs:236 (redundant_clone); crates/nexus-orchestration + crates/nexus-daemon-runtime test targets (doc_markdown, unwrap_used). 131 errors on clean base, identical count in W-1 fix worktree (clippy-neutral diff).
2026-06-17-v1.49-findings-lifecycle.note: Local clippy rust-1.93.0 is stricter than the clippy version CI/qc3 used (qc3 reported --all clean against the same code). Verified pre-existing on iteration/v1.49 @ bc8efc8d (clean base before W-1 fix) with identical 131-error count. Not a V1.49 regression. W-1 fix is clippy-neutral on the directly modified crates. Suggested fix at V1.50: pin rust-toolchain.toml to CI's resolved version, or run `cargo clippy --fix --allow-dirty --allow-staged` and address residual manually per AGENTS.md fix-cadence.

2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.title: Draft overlay narrative-indexes.md §3 lists a 4-column F### table (id|description|status|chapters) while claiming alignment with the 5-column scaffolded template (ID|Description|Planted|Paid off|Status); overlay table to be reconciled at the P5 fold into workflow-profile.md §4.6.
2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.scope: .mstar/knowledge/specs/novel-writing/narrative-indexes.md §3; runtime uses the scaffolded template 5-column shape (crates/nexus-orchestration/src/narrative_index.rs)
2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.note: No runtime behavior gap — the runtime implements the template's 5-column shape (ground truth for round-trip), and the overlay §3 4-column summary maps onto it (Planted+Paid off realize `chapters`; Status honours planned|buried|paid_off). Documentation-only reconciliation when the overlay folds into workflow-profile.md §4.6 at P-last. Also tracks the deferred promotion-concurrency advisory-lock decision (single-writer daemon model + atomic temp+rename is the V1.49 choice; a hard advisory lock is a V1.50 follow-up if multi-writer arrives).

2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.title: Intermittent flaky test `fallback_warn_includes_chapter_field` (review_report) fails under full parallel `cargo test -p nexus-orchestration`; passes in isolation. Pre-existing on iteration/v1.49 base.
2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.source: P1 implementer (pre-existing flake, verified against base per .mstar/AGENTS.md protocol)
2026-06-17-v1.49-narrative-indexes.note: Verified pre-existing per the repo's 'Pre-existing claim verification protocol': reproduces on iteration/v1.49 @ 3630a4e5 (base) with `cargo test -p nexus-orchestration` (full parallel suite — 9 passed, 1 failed in review_report); passes with `cargo test -p nexus-orchestration --test review_report` (in isolation, parallel and serial). Cause: global tracing registry contention when many test binaries install/inspect the subscriber concurrently. Not caused by V1.49 P1 — review_report.rs and its capture layer are untouched. Suggested fix: scoped subscriber guard or serializing that single test.
